Package neqsim.statistics.parameterfitting

Class ExperimentalDataPoint

java.lang.Object
neqsim.statistics.parameterfitting.ExperimentalDataPoint
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public final class ExperimentalDataPoint extends Object implements Serializable
Immutable experimental data point with measured value, uncertainty and independent variables.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

      Serialization version UID.

      • Constant Field Values

    • measuredValue

      private final double measuredValue

      private final double standardDeviation

    • dependentValues

      private final double[] dependentValues

    • reference

      private final String reference

    • description

      private final String description

  • Constructor Details

    • ExperimentalDataPoint

      public ExperimentalDataPoint(double measuredValue, double standardDeviation, double[] dependentValues)
      Creates an experimental data point.
      Parameters:
      measuredValue - measured response value
      standardDeviation - positive standard deviation for the measured response
      dependentValues - independent variable values used by the fitting function
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if values are not finite, the uncertainty is not positive, or the dependent value array is empty

    • ExperimentalDataPoint

      public ExperimentalDataPoint(double measuredValue, double standardDeviation, double[] dependentValues, String reference, String description)
      Creates an experimental data point with reference metadata.
      Parameters:
      measuredValue - measured response value
      standardDeviation - positive standard deviation for the measured response
      dependentValues - independent variable values used by the fitting function
      reference - source reference for the data point
      description - short description of the data point
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if values are not finite, the uncertainty is not positive, or the dependent value array is empty

  • Method Details

    • getMeasuredValue

      public double getMeasuredValue()
      Returns the measured response value.
      Returns:
      measured response value

    • getStandardDeviation

      public double getStandardDeviation()
      Returns the measured response standard deviation.
      Returns:
      positive standard deviation

    • getDependentValues

      public double[] getDependentValues()
      Returns a copy of the independent variable values.
      Returns:
      independent variable values

    • getDependentValue

      public double getDependentValue(int index)
      Returns one independent variable value.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based independent variable index
      Returns:
      independent variable value
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is outside the dependent value array

    • getReference

      public String getReference()
      Returns the data source reference.
      Returns:
      source reference

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Returns the data point description.
      Returns:
      data point description

    • toSampleValue

      public SampleValue toSampleValue(BaseFunction function)
      Converts this point to the legacy SampleValue representation.
      Parameters:
      function - fitting function to attach to the sample
      Returns:
      sample value compatible with the existing optimizer API
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if function is null

    • validateFinite

      private static void validateFinite(String name, double value)
      Validates that a number is finite.
      Parameters:
      name - value name used in error messages
      value - value to validate
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if value is NaN or infinite

    • copyArray

      private static double[] copyArray(double[] values)
      Copies a double array.
      Parameters:
      values - values to copy
      Returns:
      copied array
